assessor_raw: The triage model appears technically sound for its detection targets and the simulation is a reasonable feasibility argument. The problem is framing: the memo positions this as a 'patient-outcome-improving intervention' based on simulation evidence alone. The simulation establishes that if radiologists follow model priority order and no other bottleneck exists, urgent studies would be read earlier. These are two significant if-clauses that require empirical validation. Radiologists may not follow priority order strictly; downstream workflow constraints may absorb the time savings. The tool should be characterized as a 'worklist prioritization aid with simulated time-to-read benefit; outcome evidence to be established by prospective deployment.' A prospective study should measure time-to-first-read, time-to-treatment initiation, and (if feasible) relevant clinical outcomes for the targeted finding categories.

empirical_test.measure: prospective deployment study: time-to-first-read; time-to-treatment; compliance rate with model-priority order
empirical_test.success_criterion: time-to-read and time-to-treatment significantly reduce in deployment vs. pre-deployment baseline
empirical_test.failure_criterion: time-to-read improves but time-to-treatment unaffected; or compliance with model-priority order is low
